purpose: Orchestrates message summarization: applies 150-char bypass heuristic, calls LLM via Client interface, persists summaries to storage, processes a today+yesterday time-windowed queue

cross_scope_touchpoints.surface: accepts storage.Message to extract text for prompt construction
constructs storage.Message fixtures for test inputs
reads unsummarized messages and channel metadata; writes MessageSummary records
opens real SQLite store for integration-level testing; seeds Channel and Message fixtures; queries MessageSummaries for result verification

risks: HasModel performs a full ListModels round-trip on every call with no caching — callers checking model presence before each summarization batch would incur repeated HTTP overhead
http.Client created with no Timeout — only context cancellation bounds the request; a hung server with no context deadline will block indefinitely
BuildSingleMessagePrompt rejects empty message.Text but does not guard against very large texts — no truncation before sending to LLM; token limits are delegated entirely to the provider
If Channels().GetByID() returns an error or nil channel, the message is silently skipped (continue) without incrementing any counter — missed messages are invisible in the result
patterns: openRouterRequest/Message/Response/Choice types defined here are also reused by ollama.go — they represent the shared OpenAI-compatible JSON schema
Functional options pattern (ServiceOption / WithLocalModel) used for optional configuration — keeps NewService signature stable while allowing provider-specific prompt tuning
countingMockClient implements Client with a failAfter threshold, enabling deterministic partial-failure scenarios across multi-message queues
Client interface with single ChatCompletion method enables polymorphic use of OllamaClient and OpenRouterClient without import coupling
